summary: The conversation discusses methods to deter AI crawlers from websites, starting with robots.txt, then proposing a more elaborate 'dungeon' strategy to trap and identify bots for training a bot profiler.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: How can I stop AI crawlers from coming to my website?

turns.speaker: EMPLOYEE
turns.text: Just add something like this to your robots.txt with all the user agents that you don't want to be able to access your site.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: Do you think people actually care about the robots.txt file?

turns.speaker: EMPLOYEE
turns.text: Instead of just using a robots.txt, why don't you build a dungeon to get them stuck and waste their time?

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: Okay, but how do we decide if somebody gets the dungeon or not?

turns.speaker: EMPLOYEE
turns.text: Start by identifying if a user is a bot or not. You can use things like their user agent, their IP address, and their traffic pattern. This is difficult at first, but it will get easier over time.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: Makes sense. What's in the dungeon, and how do we build it?

turns.speaker: EMPLOYEE
turns.text: Hundreds or thousands of pages of real-looking static HTML content about real stuff, just generic useless information.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: How does someone go from our regular content to the dungeon, though?

turns.speaker: EMPLOYEE
turns.text: When a suspicious user requests a page, we add invisible anchor links that no human would ever click or see to the web page. Those anchor tags lead to our pre-generated content dungeon.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: Okay, but beyond wasting their time, money, and compute, what's the point of this?

turns.speaker: EMPLOYEE
turns.text: Remember the first step, deciding if someone's a bot or not? This is difficult if you don't have data, but the dungeon makes this easy. Anybody that gets stuck in the dungeon is probably a bot, so we can use that to train our profiler to catch bots more quickly in the future.

turns.speaker: INTERVIEWER
turns.text: That's pretty sick. Does anybody do this already?
